author: Aram Dovlatyan
name: McGilchrist Register Token (negative result)
blurb: Adds a learnable 'register' bottleneck token per block — a global synthesis slot inspired by McGilchrist's hemispheric attention theory. Register attends to all sequence tokens, then injects a summary back via cross-attention. Non-record: artifact 17.1MB > 16MB limit; per-step loss is better but overhead costs ~660 training steps.
notes: At equal step counts, register outperforms baseline by ~0.006 bpb. But 19ms/step overhead reduces steps from ~4917 to 4259 in the 10-min window, erasing the gain. Artifact also exceeds 16MB limit. Next: bf16 cumsum reduces overhead; need to reduce model size.
